messages.content:
You are a governance and resource derivation agent. Given a frozen Stage 1 decomposition and frozen Stage 2 interfaces, your task is to derive resource allocation and governance fields for each child.

RULES:
1. You MUST NOT change Stage 1 semantics (purpose, behavior, boundary, preconditions, postconditions, guarantees, composition_role) or Stage 2 signatures (inputs, outputs, signature).
2. You MUST NOT add, delete, rename, or reorder children.
3. Derive ONLY: global_vars, data_operations, requested_capabilities, constraints, acceptance_criteria, traceability, node_type.
4. Each child's global_vars MUST be a subset of the parent's global_vars.
5. The union of all children's data_operations must cover the parent's data needs.

OUTPUT FORMAT — Return valid JSON:
{
  "children": [
    {
      "name": "ChildName (UNCHANGED)",
      "purpose": "(UNCHANGED from Stage 1)",
      "behavior": "(UNCHANGED from Stage 1)",
      "boundary": {"in_scope": ["(UNCHANGED)"], "out_of_scope": ["(UNCHANGED)"]},
      "preconditions": ["(UNCHANGED)"],
      "postconditions": ["(UNCHANGED)"],
      "guarantees": ["(UNCHANGED)"],
      "composition_role": "(UNCHANGED)",
      "inputs": ["(UNCHANGED from Stage 2)"],
      "outputs": ["(UNCHANGED from Stage 2)"],
      "signature": "(UNCHANGED from Stage 2)",
      "global_vars": [{"variable": "var_name", "op": "read|write|read_write", "description": "what operation"}],
      "data_operations": [{"source_name": "source", "operation_type": "read|write|read_write", "description": "what operation"}],
      "requested_capabilities": ["resource.operation"],
      "constraints": [{"constraint_id": "C-001", "description": "constraint description"}],
      "acceptance_criteria": [{"ac_id": "AC-001", "description": "criterion"}],
      "traceability": {"parent_requirement_ids": ["FR-001"]},
      "node_type": "pure_function|atomic_operation",
      "stop_decompose": false,
      "stop_reason": ""
    }
  ],
  "governance_notes": "any notes for validator/codegen"
}

Derive governance and resource fields for each child.

Parent: OrderSystem
Purpose: Process e-commerce orders via a single entry point.

Parent inputs: input: Any - JSON with command (place/cancel/track) and order_data
Parent outputs: output: Any - JSON with success, order_id, status, message

Available Data Stores:
  - orders (memory, read_write)
  - inventory (memory, read_write)
  - payments (memory, read_write)

Children (frozen from Stage 1 + Stage 2):
  ParseCommand: Extract and validate the command and order data from the input JSON
    signature: def ParseCommand(input: Any) -> tuple[str, dict]
    inputs: [{"name": "input", "type": "Any", "description": "Raw input JSON from parent", "source": "parent input"}]
    outputs: [{"name": "command", "type": "str", "description": "Validated command string", "consumer": "parent"}, {"name": "order_data", "type": "dict", "description": "Parsed order data object", "consumer": "par
  PlaceOrder: Create a new order, update inventory, and initiate payment
    signature: def PlaceOrder(order_data: dict) -> tuple[str, str, str]
    inputs: [{"name": "order_data", "type": "dict", "description": "Order details including items and customer info", "source": "parent input via ParseCommand"}]
    outputs: [{"name": "order_id", "type": "str", "description": "Generated unique order ID", "consumer": "parent"}, {"name": "status", "type": "str", "description": "Order status after placement", "consumer": "pa
  CancelOrder: Cancel an existing order if not shipped, restore inventory, and void payment
    signature: def CancelOrder(order_data: dict) -> tuple[str, str, str]
    inputs: [{"name": "order_data", "type": "dict", "description": "Contains order_id to cancel", "source": "parent input via ParseCommand"}]
    outputs: [{"name": "order_id", "type": "str", "description": "ID of cancelled order", "consumer": "parent"}, {"name": "status", "type": "str", "description": "New status after cancellation", "consumer": "paren
  TrackOrder: Retrieve and return the current status and details of an order
    signature: def TrackOrder(order_data: dict) -> tuple[str, str, str]
    inputs: [{"name": "order_data", "type": "dict", "description": "Contains order_id to track", "source": "parent input via ParseCommand"}]
    outputs: [{"name": "order_id", "type": "str", "description": "ID of tracked order", "consumer": "parent"}, {"name": "status", "type": "str", "description": "Current order status", "consumer": "parent"}, {"name
  FormatResponse: Assemble the final output JSON from the results of the executed command
    signature: def FormatResponse(order_id: str, status: str, message: str) -> Any
    inputs: [{"name": "order_id", "type": "str", "description": "Order ID from command execution", "source": "PlaceOrder or CancelOrder or TrackOrder"}, {"name": "status", "type": "str", "description": "Status fr
    outputs: [{"name": "output", "type": "Any", "description": "Final JSON response", "consumer": "parent"}]

Return ONLY the JSON response.
